channel.title: PodIntel Daily Briefing
channel.description: A daily podcast episode that tells you what's worth listening to across all your podcasts. Your feed, summarized in under 4 minutes.
channel.itunes:author: PodIntel
channel.itunes:summary: A daily podcast episode that tells you what's worth listening to across all your podcasts. Your feed, summarized in under 4 minutes.

item.title: Friday Apr 3 — 5 episodes | Tariff refund mess, investment banking, stolen KitKats
item.description:
🔥 GEM: The Journal — How Do You Refund $166 Billion? (18m) → [07:25] "We live in the age of computers" — Judge Eaton to the government → [09:03] Ruling from the bench: start refunding now → [12:05] Costco sued by consumers wanting refund share
✓ NOTABLE: Odd Lots — What Investment Bankers Actually Do All Day (54m) → [05:07] Scott Bok started on Wall Street in 1981 when M&A barely existed → [08:15] "The real job is managing CEO egos during deals"
✓ NOTABLE: Finshots Daily — The Economics of Stolen KitKats (8m) → 12,000 kilos vanished between Italy and Poland → Food is 22% of all cargo theft — none shows up in GDP
⏭ SKIP: Bloomberg Daybreak — Bank earnings preview. Nothing beyond headlines. ⏭ SKIP: MSSP Ep 606 — The usual chaos.
item.itunes:summary: The Journal has a fascinating story about a 77-year-old judge handling $166B in tariff refunds. Odd Lots explains what investment bankers actually do. Finshots covers stolen KitKats economics.
